text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village somewhere in England there lived a farmer named Ebenezer. Ebenezer was unusual—not just because he loved parrots. You see, despite being lonely, he couldn't help feeling immensely proud whenever his feathered friend parrots words.

One crisp autumn morning, while munching on his favorite porridge, Ebenezer noticed something peculiar. His parrot didn't just whistle happily. It started a small poem:

"Together we fly,
Over fluffy white snowy oats.
One bird does the whole show,
With wings that just gleach."

Ebenezer chuckled at his own feathered friend. Over Sundays, he often read poetry out loud, usually just to himself. But now it felt like heaven. Every owl call, every rustling leaf felt like a tiny stanza.

Days turned into weeks. Every dawn seemed just a bit brighter with a poem. Ebenezer began writing them down himself. Soon enough, villagers began stumbling upon these poems. Tiny
